calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Continued To Jul-01-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS Y COMBINATOR MANAGEMENT, LLC, YC AFFILIATES FUND II, LP, YC AFFILIATES FUND II (QP), LP'S DEMURRER TO OSWALD'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances via Zoom:
Ryan Landes of Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, LLP (213.443.3145, ryanlandes@quinnemanuel.com) for defendants and moving parties;
Harmony Oswald (408.825.4529, hno@oswaldfirm.com) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: sustained without leave to amend as to the first through sixth causes of action for fraud and overruled as to the ninth cause of action for negligence and the tenth cause of action for statutory unfair competition.

By her express disclaimer that the eight causes of action that are the subject of this demurrer do not concern acquisition, use or disclosure of her alleged confidential or trade secret information, none of those causes of action are preempted by CUTSA.

Defendants' statute of limitations and conflation of defendants arguments have been previously rejected and will not be entertained again.

The six fraud causes of action fail to allege any actionable misrepresentations, false promises, concealment, or justifiable reliance. The essence of each of those claims is that defendants allegedly promised to "carefully review" and give Oswald's application a "fair, good-faith evaluation," falsely represented that they "fairly declined" the application and "concealed the actual nature of its intake process which was unfair and careless." These alleged fraudulent acts are non-actionable opinions about the quality of defendants' conduct and are not the type of acts that can be justifiably relied on. Gentry v. eBay, Inc. (2002) 99 Cal. App. 4th 816, 835). Because her third amended complaint was Oswald's final opportunity to allege actionable fraud claims and she does not offer any basis for a reasonable possibility that she can allege any viable fraud claims, the demurrer to the first six claims are sustained without leave to amend.

Liberally construing Oswald's negligence and section 17200 claims, as is required on this demurrer, those claims are sufficiently pled. On her negligence claim, Oswald alleges a duty by defendants to exercise reasonable diligence and care during the intake process, the breach of that duty, causation and damage. On her 17200 claim, she alleges that defendants acted unfairly by "actively discouraging entrepreneurs from using written legal protections" for their protectable information. Those allegations are enough to survive a demurrer. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR 13293 (415.274.9977, calendar-la@veritext.com) (301/HEK)

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS Y COMBINATOR MANAGEMENT, LLC, Y COMBINATOR ES20, LLC, Y COMBINATOR ES24, LLC, YC AFFILIATES FUND II, L.P., YC AFFILIATES FUND II(QP), L.P., YC AFFILIATES FUND IIA, L.P.'s DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

California Rules of Court, Rule 3.113, subdivision (d), caps the length of an opposing brief on demurrer at 15 pages. Plaintiff's brief is 33 pages of substantive briefing excluding the table of contents. The court strikes plaintiff's overlength brief in its entirety and continues this matter for hearing to October 20, 2025. Plaintiff may file a new opposition brief that conforms to the Rules of Court by no later than nine court days in advance of the continued hearing date. Defendants may submit a new reply brief; if they elect not to file a new reply brief, the court will consider their already-filed reply brief as their reply.

The court cautions plaintiff to adhere carefully to applicable Rules of Court in future filings and may strike future overlength briefs without granting an opportunity to cure. All parties, including self-represented ones, are held to the Rules of Court and the Code of Civil Procedure.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
